risk.desc: Potential material fatigue due to high cyclic loading on the harmonic drive.
risk.mit: Implement stress relief features and conduct fatigue analysis.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage due to inadequate strain relief at cable entry points.
risk.mit: Ensure strain relief features are included and cable routing respects minimum bend radius.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal stress due to uneven heat distribution.
risk.mit: Optimize cooling channels and conduct thermal analysis.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to lack of clear alignment features.
risk.mit: Include clear alignment features and detailed assembly instructions.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient structural integrity due to thin wall sections.
risk.mit: Review wall thicknesses and conduct structural analysis.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to high heat dissipation from the motor and harmonic drive.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management features such as heat sinks and cooling channels.

requirements.spec: Elbow pitch joint J3, +-150deg, cycloidal reducer, 50Nm, encoder dual-stack
requirements.description: A 150-degree pitch elbow joint for a 6-DOF industrial arm with a cycloidal reducer and 50Nm torque capacity, featuring a dual-stack encoder for position feedback.

requirements.spec: Vehicle context: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach
requirements.description: The elbow joint is part of a 6-DOF robotic arm designed to handle payloads up to 5kg with a reach of 600mm.

requirements.spec: Suggested material: Al 7075 + steel + composite, process: CNC + LPBF
requirements.description: The joint is to be manufactured using a combination of aluminum 7075, steel, and composite materials, with a manufacturing process that includes CNC machining and LPBF (Laser Powder Bed Fusion) for complex internal features.

risk.desc: The mass estimate is slightly higher than expected, which may affect the overall vehicle mass budget.
risk.mit: Review the material selection and manufacturing process to optimize for weight reduction.

risk.desc: The thermal dissipation from the reducer to the arm link may be insufficient, leading to overheating.
risk.mit: Increase the thermal conductivity of the material or add additional cooling channels.

risk.desc: The cable routing may be too tight, leading to potential strain on the cables during motion.
risk.mit: Review the cable routing and add additional slack loops or strain relief components.

risk.desc: The service access panel may not provide sufficient access for maintenance and inspection.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the service access panel or add additional access points.

risk.desc: The fastener pattern may not provide sufficient preload, leading to potential joint failure.
risk.mit: Review the fastener pattern and add additional locking mechanisms or increase the preload.
